hero_title_accent: Secure Sovereign Infrastructure Without Vendor Lock-In
hero_subtitle: SFTY provides government agencies a multi-provider platform with role-based access, audit logging, and phased deployment designed to protect data sovereignty and reduce vendor dependency.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market
trust_card_3_value: Originator and beneficiary data checked for completeness
trust_card_5_value: On-premise or private cloud for sensitive data
trust_card_6_value: Supervisory outputs, evidence and regulatory reports
problem_title: Procurement slows when vendor lock persists
problem_description: Government teams face delays and complexity when supervisory infrastructure depends on a single provider. This creates challenges for phased implementation, data sovereignty, secure deployment and consistent audit logging within regulated environments.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY enables government teams to implement a multi-provider supervisory layer with controlled access, secure audit logging, and modular integration that respects data sovereignty and procurement timelines.
workflow_title: From deployment setup to audit logs
workflow_description: SFTY supports phased deployment with role-based access, secure evidence logging and audit trails that maintain sovereign control over supervisory workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another vendor lock-in. Secure sovereign deployment
why_sfty_description: SFTY provides regulators a sovereign, multi-provider orchestration layer that preserves data sovereignty, audit logs, and role-based control without tying them to a single vendor.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY enables regulators to maintain secure, auditable, and governed supervisory environments with controlled access and procurement-ready deployment from day one.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: How does SFTY support sovereign deployment without vendor lock-in
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how SFTY enables phased rollout in government environments
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit logs preserve evidence for supervisory review
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe multi-provider orchestration for flexible regulatory workflows
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for government regulators?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides regulators with a secure supervisory layer that connects VASP data, licensing, wallets, transactions, alerts, cases and reporting into one integrated environment.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support multi-provider orchestration?
faq_2_answer: SFTY aggregates outputs from multiple KYT, KYC, Travel Rule and analytics providers into structured workflows, preserving evidence and enabling comparison without vendor lock-in.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve supervisory evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, decision logs and evidence-grade audit records within a controlled environment to support defensible supervisory actions and review readiness.
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY supports on-premise and private cloud deployment with role-based access control, secure governance and integration in sovereign government environments.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing supervisory tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY complements existing supervisory and analytics tools by structuring their outputs into connected, auditable workflows without replacing them.
final_title: Review secure sovereign deployment with SFTY
final_description: Explore how SFTY supports phased deployment, role-based access and multi-provider orchestration for government supervision.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.

footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
